business.headline: Sensex, Nifty open flat as crude oil prices weigh in on investor sentimentThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 63.36 points to 75,566.21, while the NSE Nifty50 added 21.90 points to 23,430.70 as of 9:42 am.

Gold prices drop despite ongoing West Asia war. What's going on?It is unusual for gold to fall during volatile situations like war. But that is exactly what is happening now and it raises a key question about what is driving prices today

Tenant vs landlord: How India's new rental rules change the balanceRenting a home in India has long been a game of negotiation, where rules were often unclear and power rarely equal between tenants and landlords. A new rental framework based on the Model Tenancy Act, 2021 is trying to formalise how homes are rented in India.

Live: 610 cylinders seized in Delhi as crackdown on hoarders intensifiesLPG crisis live updates: After the arrival of the Shivalik vessel, another LPG carrier, Nanda Devi, is set to reach Gujarat's Vadinar port on Saturday morning with over 47,000 metric tonnes of LPG, offering further relief to domestic supplies. Part of the cargo is expected to be sent to Tamil Nadu as distribution begins. Even as fresh shipments arrive, the impact of supply disruptions continues to be felt across sectors. Workplaces in cities like Pune are seeing strain, with IT employees seeking work-from-home options as office cafeterias operate with limited menus. The government has stepped up efforts to ease pressure on LPG by urging commercial users to shift to piped natural gas, with companies offering incentives to encourage PNG adoption. Authorities also continue to monitor supply and crack down on hoarding and misinformation, while maintaining that there is no nationwide shortage. Follow this live blog for the latest updates on LPG supply, government measures and the situation across cities and industries in India.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market fall or rise today?Early signals suggest a positive start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,515.50 at around 8:00 am. This points to the Nifty 50 opening above Monday's closing level of 23,408.8.

How young professionals are piling up Rs 10–50 lakh debt through small EMIsIt begins with a small EMI—a new phone, a quick getaway, but for many young professionals, these tiny payments are quietly adding up to tens of lakhs. Are they prepared to handle it?

Gold drops over Rs 3,400, silver cheaper by Rs 11,000: Is it time to invest?Despite global tensions, gold and silver turned cheaper, with gold down Rs 3,400 and silver down Rs 11,000. Does this decline signal a good entry point for investors?

Government urges LPG users to switch to PNG as companies roll out incentivesSujata Sharma, Joint Secretary in the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, said the move has been discussed over the past few days as authorities look to ensure stable fuel availability across the country.
Sensex ends 939 points higher, Nifty above 23,300; SBI up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex was up 938.93 points to end at 75,502.85, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 257.70 points to close at 23,408.80.

Saudi Arabia offers alternate Red Sea route as Strait of Hormuz faces crisisSaudi Arabia is now giving its long-term customers the option to receive their April oil allocations through the Red Sea port of Yanbu instead of the usual Persian Gulf terminals.

Citigroup lowers Nifty target to 27,000 amid war risksThe global brokerage has cut its Nifty target to 27,000 from the earlier estimate of 28,500. Even after the reduction, the new target still suggests a possible upside of about 17% from the Nifty's last closing level.

Buying motor insurance? Why the fine print may matter more than the premiumMotor insurance is intended to shift risk from households. Mis-selling this type of insurance has the reverse effect, leaving the consumer vulnerable at the very time they are seeking protection.

PhonePe delays IPO as Iran conflict cools primary marketPhonePe said on Monday that it has temporarily deferred its public market listing due to rising uncertainty in global markets linked to the conflict in West Asia. The company added that it will restart the process once conditions in capital markets become more stable.

Bandhan Bank share price crashes 10%: Why is the stock down today?Bandhan Bank's stock was trading at around Rs 159.50 at about 1.50 pm, down 9.19% on the National Stock Exchange (BSE). Read to know why the shares of the bank tumbled today.

Central Mine Planning IPO: Price set at Rs 172. Worth subscribing as per GMP?The Central Mine Planning IPO is a book-built issue and the entire offer consists of an offer for sale. A total of 10.71 crore shares will be sold in the issue, taking the total size of the IPO to Rs 1,842.12 crore.

IDBI Bank share price crashes 16%: Why is the stock down today?A 16% crash in IDBI Bank shares has caught the market's attention. Such sharp moves rarely happen without a reason, leaving investors asking what exactly went wrong today.

How many pensioners are under OPS and NPS? Govt shares numbersThere has been growing discussion around OPS and NPS in recent years, especially among government employees. The Centre has now revealed the latest numbers of pensioners under both schemes in Parliament. So how many pensioners are covered under OPS and NPS?

Why has the Income Tax Department asked taxpayers to ignore advance tax emails?Many taxpayers were surprised to receive emails about advance tax payments mentioning "significant transactions". But soon after complaints surfaced, the Income Tax Department asked people to ignore those messages. So what exactly went wrong?

Buying the market dip? Why index funds may be the smarter betMarkets will rise and fall, but discipline often decides long-term returns. For investors unsure about timing the next move, index funds offer a simple way to stay invested and ride the market. Here's why they could be worth a closer look.

Sensex jumps 1,000 points despite Iran war. Why is the stock market rising today?The BSE Sensex surged during the session and was trading at 75,802.18 at around 2:55 pm, up 1,238.26 points or 1.66%. The NSE Nifty also moved higher and was trading near 23,493.15, up 342.05 points.

entertainment.headline: Sean Penn skips Oscars to visit Zelenskyy in war-hit Kyiv after third win Hollywood actor Sean Penn skipped the Oscars after winning Best Supporting Actor to visit Ukrainian President Zelenskyy in war-torn Kyiv, showing his steadfast support for Ukraine amid ongoing conflict.

Dhurandhar 2 report: Rs 123 crore in pre-sales globally, post-midnight shows added Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge is making history with a massive Rs 123 crore in global pre-sales.  Reports suggest that multiplexes are scheduling post-midnight shows for the film.

Mere Sai writers allege unpaid dues worth lakhs, accuse producers of going silent The writers of the popular 2023 television show Mere Sai complained of unpaid dues by the production house. In an exclusive interview, the writers shared that their payment have been pending for three years.

Besties about The Ton: How Bridgerton redefined friendship goals Beyond the sweeping romances, Bridgerton's latest season celebrates the friendships that truly matter. From Sophie and Alfie's loyal bond to the unbreakable ties among the ton's matriarchs, these relationships stand just as strong as any love story.

BTS: The Return trailer reaffirms long-awaited comeback as a global cultural reset Pop sensation BTS unveiled the trailer for BTS: The Return, highlighting their emotional journey after four years. The documentary is centred on reunion and resilience, featuring the traditional Korean folk song Arirang.

Dune 3: Zendaya-Timothee return, Robert Pattinson joins cast; see new posters The posters of Dune: Part 3 are out. The new instalment marks the entry of Robert Pattinson, who is believed to be playing the villain.

Chiraiya asks the uncomfortable marital rape question: Does marriage cancel consent? In Chiraiya, a small-town woman begins to question the rules she has lived by all her life. As Divya Dutta's Kamles confronts uncomfortable truths, the show asks a difficult question: does marriage erase consent?

Bruno Mars dismisses claims of shading Taylor Swift: Only love for her Singer Bruno Mars reacted to accidentally liking a reel on Instagram that called Taylor Swift "talentless." In a social media post, he praised the singer and wrote how much love and respect he holds for her.

Vir Das on why India rarely has 'Gervais or Conan-style' hosting at film awards Vir Das shared why roast-style hosting like Ricky Gervais or Conan O'Brien at international award shows rarely works in India. He said star egos and industry power dynamics make it difficult for comedians to openly joke about top actors.

Shreya Ghoshal says she rejected Dabangg 2 hit Fevicol Se: Too much objectification Shreya Ghoshal shared that she refused to sing the popular song Fevicol Se from Dabangg 2 because she felt the lyrics were too objectifying.

Actor Jannat Zubair, brother Ayaan safe after alleged assault and highway chase Jannat Zubair and her brother Ayaan Zubair are safe after an alleged physical assault and highway chase incident in Panvel, Mumbai. In a statement, her team confirmed that the police are investigating the matter.

Rapper Raftaar says he has no connection to Rajkummar Rao, Keerthy Suresh's Raftaar Rapper Raftaar clarified that he has no connection to the upcoming film Raftaar starring Rajkummar Rao and Keerthy Suresh. The clarification came after some fans assumed the film might be linked to the rapper.

Ireland celebrates Jessie Buckley as country's first Best Actress Oscar winner Jessie Buckley created history by becoming the first Irish Best Actress Oscar winner. President Catherine Connolly congratulated Buckley on her achievement.

Even Vijju was Team Bride: Rashmika Mandanna, Vijay Deverakonda share Haldi pics Vijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna shared colourful glimpses from their Haldi ceremony. Rashmika shared that the celebration felt like Haldi + Holi and was filled with games, colours, and family traditions.

Apoorva Lakhia on why Salman Khan's war drama was renamed Maatrubhumi Apoorva Lakhia opened up on the reason behind Salman Khan's Battle of Galwan being renamed Maatrubhumi. He said that the title change was not an "overnight decision."

Viral: Michael B Jordan hugs Leonardo DiCaprio after winning Best Actor Michael B Jordan won his first Best Actor Oscar for his film Sinners at the 98th Academy Awards. After the win, fellow nominee Leonardo DiCaprio congratulated him, and the two shared a warm hug.

Hema Malini calls Dharmendra's omission from Oscars In Memoriam a 'shame' Hema Malini expressed disappointment after Dharmendra was left out of the televised In Memoriam segment at the Oscars. Calling the omission a "shame," she said the late actor was loved by audiences across the world.

Kanye West's New Delhi concert gets a new date amid global tensions Kanye West's highly anticipated concert in New Delhi on March 29 has been postponed due to rising global tensions. Organisers have assured fans that all tickets remain valid for the new date.

Armaan Malik slams Nora Fatehi's Sarke Chunar Teri Sarke lyrics Nora Fatehi's song Sarke Chunar Teri Sarke from KD: The Devil has sparked backlash online over its lyrics. Armaan Malik called the track "a new low".

general news.headline: We don't need anybody: Trump fumes as allies snub call to join Hormuz missionMany of the United States' key allies have rejected President Donald Trump's call to deploy warships to the Strait of Hormuz to reopen the strategic oil route blocked by Iran.

400 killed, 250 wounded after Pakistan airstrike hits Kabul hospital: TalibanAfghan officials said the strike destroyed a drug treatment hospital in Kabul and left hundreds injured, while Pakistan denied targeting civilians and said its airstrikes hit militant infrastructure.

Mojtaba Khamenei cheated death by seconds in strike that killed fatherAccording to a report by The Telegraph, Israeli ballistic missiles struck the compound soon after Mojtaba left the building, killing Ali Khamenei along with dozens of senior officials and relatives.

Bhai, kya kardiya tune: Friend records as Delhi man shoots himself in the chestThe man loaded the magazine, cocked the pistol, briefly looked at the camera, and then shot himself in the left side of his chest.

You Indian? Get lost: Are Indians treated badly in Vietnam?Vietnam has grown popular among Indian travellers, but some say they faced hostility in shops and markets. Tourists and locals suggest stereotypes about Indians may be contributing to the friction.

Trump stunned by intelligence claim that Mojtaba Khamenei may be gay: ReportAccording to a report by the New York Post, US intelligence suggested Mojtaba may have had a long-term relationship with a man who served as his childhood tutor. The news outlet said the claim was revealed to it by two intelligence community officials and a person close to the White House.

How Indian expats had the back of stranded Indians in war-hit UAEDozens of Indian travellers were stuck as flights from Dubai airport got cancelled with the Middle East turned into a war zone amid the US-Israel-Iran war. Indian expats opened their homes and farmhouses in the UAE for fellow Indians.

Live: Intel warned Trump Iran could retaliate against Gulf alliesUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Middle East conflict continues to escalate with fresh developments across the region. US President Donald Trump said Washington was "shocked" after Iran began striking Gulf countries including Kuwait and Bahrain, widening the war's scope. Meanwhile, tensions have intensified around the Strait of Hormuz as several US allies declined Trump's call to deploy warships to escort oil tankers. Israel also reported new Iranian missile launches, while global markets remain on edge amid fears of prolonged disruption to energy supplies. Follow indiatoday.in for the latest live updates.

Dhurandhar 2 report: Rs 123 crore in pre-sales globally, post-midnight shows addedRanveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge is making history with a massive Rs 123 crore in global pre-sales.  Reports suggest that multiplexes are scheduling post-midnight shows for the film.

Is oral sex replacing smoking as the leading cause of throat cancer?While smoking remains the leading cause of throat cancer in India, HPV infections transmitted through oral sex have overtaken tobacco as the primary cause in countries such as the US and the UK.

Live: 610 cylinders seized in Delhi as crackdown on hoarders intensifiesLPG crisis live updates: After the arrival of the Shivalik vessel, another LPG carrier, Nanda Devi, is set to reach Gujarat's Vadinar port on Saturday morning with over 47,000 metric tonnes of LPG, offering further relief to domestic supplies. Part of the cargo is expected to be sent to Tamil Nadu as distribution begins. Even as fresh shipments arrive, the impact of supply disruptions continues to be felt across sectors. Workplaces in cities like Pune are seeing strain, with IT employees seeking work-from-home options as office cafeterias operate with limited menus. The government has stepped up efforts to ease pressure on LPG by urging commercial users to shift to piped natural gas, with companies offering incentives to encourage PNG adoption. Authorities also continue to monitor supply and crack down on hoarding and misinformation, while maintaining that there is no nationwide shortage. Follow this live blog for the latest updates on LPG supply, government measures and the situation across cities and industries in India.

NDA wins all 5 Rajya Sabha seats in Bihar, 2 in Odisha; Congress cries foulThe NDA won all five Rajya Sabha seats from Bihar after four Opposition MLAs skipped voting, dealing a setback to the Mahagathbandhan. The alliance also secured two seats in Odisha amid cross-voting by Opposition MLAs during the biennial polls.

Chiraiya asks the uncomfortable marital rape question: Does marriage cancel consent?In Chiraiya, a small-town woman begins to question the rules she has lived by all her life. As Divya Dutta's Kamles confronts uncomfortable truths, the show asks a difficult question: does marriage erase consent?

Biased: India slams 'selective targeting' as US body seeks sanctions on RAW, RSSResponding to media queries, Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said the government "categorically rejects" the report and its characterisation of India. He said the commission has repeatedly presented a "distorted and selective picture" of the country.

A war crime: Afghanistan cricketers slam deadly Pak strikes on Kabul hospitalAfghanistan cricket stars Rashid Khan and Mohammad Nabi have strongly condemned reported Pakistani airstrikes in Kabul. The two players expressed grief and anger over civilian casualties after a hospital was reportedly struck during the attack.

Start cholesterol drugs as early as your 30s, say new guidelinesThe recommendations highlight a growing consensus among experts: the earlier high cholesterol is treated, the better the long-term health outcomes.

The workplace runs on male biology. And women are expected to keep upThe debate around menstrual leave has resurfaced after the Supreme Court said mandating it may discourage companies from hiring women. But the controversy raises a deeper question: why are women still expected to ignore biological realities and work within systems designed almost entirely around male physiology?

Sri Lanka shifts to 4-day work week to conserve oil stocks amid Hormuz crisisSri Lanka has moved to a four-day work week to conserve fuel as the Middle East war disrupts oil supplies through the Strait of Hormuz, while Bangladesh has begun rationing fuel and Pakistan has shifted offices to remote work to ease an emerging energy crunch.

A mechanic's advice kills 4 members of Andhra Pradesh family. Here's what happenedThe family, a resident of Thyagaraju Street in Punganur town, had recently repaired the engine of their two-wheeler vehicle. A local mechanic advised the family to keep the engine running overnight to "set" the newly installed parts. However, this turned out to be fatal for the family.

Vir Das on why India rarely has 'Gervais or Conan-style' hosting at film awardsVir Das shared why roast-style hosting like Ricky Gervais or Conan O'Brien at international award shows rarely works in India. He said star egos and industry power dynamics make it difficult for comedians to openly joke about top actors.

Saviour to Satan: Biography of Iran–US hostilityThe story began with America as Iran's saviour but ended with America as its Satan. The rivalry written over decades in oil and blood is catastrophically being settled with fire that can singe the world.

Bengaluru cafe charges customer 5% 'gas crisis' fee on lemonade bill amid LPG crisisIn a post on X, a user shared a picture of the receipt from Bengaluru's Theo Cafe, which shows a 5 per cent "gas crisis charge" of Rs 17.01 applied to an order of two mint lemonades priced at Rs 179 each. The beverages together cost Rs 358.

How young professionals are piling up Rs 10–50 lakh debt through small EMIsIt begins with a small EMI—a new phone, a quick getaway, but for many young professionals, these tiny payments are quietly adding up to tens of lakhs. Are they prepared to handle it?

Doctors remove 1-litre plastic bottle from 38-year-old man's rectum in AgraAccording to health officials, the man had been suffering from severe pain for nearly 36 hours before his family brought him to a private hospital. The surgery to remove the bottle lasted for 1 hour and 10 minutes.

sports.headline: IPL as big as international cricket: Ricky Ponting explains India's huge talent pool Ricky Ponting believes the Indian Premier League plays a major role in shaping India's T20 success. The former Australia captain said IPL matches are as intense as international games and help prepare players for the national team.

Neymar focused on FIFA World Cup dream despite another Brazil squad snub Neymar's hopes of playing at the 2026 FIFA World Cup remain alive despite another Brazil squad snub. The Santos forward admitted he was disappointed after being left out of Brazil's upcoming friendlies in the United States.

Watch: Rohit Sharma in top gear in MI nets session ahead of IPL 2026 IPL 2026: Rohit Sharma has already started turning heads ahead of IPL 2026. A viral video from Mumbai Indians' training session showed the former India captain going after the bowlers, raising excitement among fans before the new season.

A war crime: Afghanistan cricketers slam deadly Pak strikes on Kabul hospital Afghanistan cricket stars Rashid Khan and Mohammad Nabi have strongly condemned reported Pakistani airstrikes in Kabul. The two players expressed grief and anger over civilian casualties after a hospital was reportedly struck during the attack.

KKR's fast bowling problem: 2024 winners head towards shaky start to IPL campaign Kolkata Knight Riders enter the new IPL season with a fast-bowling unit already battered by injuries and uncertainty. With Harshit Rana unlikely to feature and Matheesha Pathirana still recovering, the defending champions suddenly look short of pace options and experience.

Pratika Rawal opens up on her deep bond with Test cricket: This is my format Pratika Rawal said that she considers Test cricket the ultimate measure of a cricketer's character, patience, and determination. She made her debut in the Day-Night Perth Test against Australia, scoring her maiden fifty.

Chennai Super Kings appoint ex-England wicketkeeper as fielding coach for IPL 2026 Chennai Super Kings have appointed former England wicketkeeper-batter James Foster as their new fielding coach ahead of IPL 2026, adding an experienced coach to Stephen Fleming's support staff.

Liam Livingstone slams Brendon McCullum, Rob Key in sensational rant against ECB Liam Livingstone launched a blistering attack on England's team management, accusing Brendon McCullum and Rob Key of poor communication and a lack of support after being dropped from the white-ball setup.

The story of how Suryakumar, not Hardik Pandya, became India's T20I captain Suryakumar Yadav was made the T20I captain after the 2024 World Cup, pipping the then vice-captain Hardik Pandya. The batter reveals how he got to know about the news.

Vaishnavi Adkar continues dream run, closes in on India's No.1 WTA ranking Vaishnavi Adkar's rapid rise continued as she broke into the WTA top 400 for the first time, climbing to a career-best No. 397 after winning the ITF W35 Kalaburagi title and moving within six spots of India's No.1 ranking.

Pakistan fast bowler grabs limelight with massive no-ball in National T20 Cup Pakistan pacer Asad Akhtar grabbed attention after bowling a huge no-ball during Karachi Blues' National T20 Cup clash against Lahore Blues on Saturday at the Imran Khan Cricket Stadium in Peshawar.

Chelsea fined 131 crore rupees, 1-year-ban for breaching financial rulesChelsea have been fined $14.3 million and handed a suspended transfer ban after the Premier League found the club guilty of breaching financial regulations. Undisclosed payments, made between 2011 and 2018 have landed the club in trouble.

It is not Shubman Gill: Fans slam BCCI over huge mistake in Naman AwardsThe Indian Cricket Board is being called out for an extremely silly mistake at the Naman Awards 2026. During the ceremony, BCCI credited Shubman Gill as the captain who won the Champions Trophy, and not Rohit Sharma.

Antonelli arrives, Hamilton makes comeback in scintillating Shanghai showdownShanghai GP: Kimi Antonelli's maiden win, Lewis Hamilton's Ferrari debut podium, and Max Verstappen's frustration headlined a thrilling Chinese Grand Prix as Formula 1's 2026 season bursts into life in Shanghai.

MS Dhoni always has a positive influence on me: Sanju Samson eager for CSK stintSanju Samson said he is excited to share the CSK dressing room with MS Dhoni in IPL 2026, adding that the former India captain has always had a positive influence on him and will be a valuable source of learning.

Unleash the real Hardik Pandya: Harbhajan Singh on what MI needs to win IPL 2026IPL 2026: Harbhajan Singh believes that the Mumbai Indians need to find a way to bring out the best in Hardik Pandya should they stand a chance of getting back to winning titles.

Ricky Ponting explains the bold call that shaped India's T20 WC title defenceRicky Ponting said India's bold call to restore Sanju Samson as opener and move Ishan Kishan to No. 3 played a major role in their successful T20 World Cup 2026 title defence.

Fastest IPL hundred or Chris Gayle's 175? Vaibhav Sooryavanshi makes his pickDuring the Naman Awards on Sunday, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi revealed that he aspires to break Chris Gayle's long-standing record of 175, the highest individual score in T20 cricket.

Buying Pak players contributes to death of Indian soldiers: GavaskarSunil Gavaskar has criticised SunRisers Leeds owners for signing Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed at The Hundred 2026 auction, arguing that Indian owners must not contribute to Pakistani government revenues.

No India T20 World Cup cricketer nominated for February's Player of the Month awardDespite India's successful T20 World Cup title defence, no Indian men's cricketer was shortlisted for the ICC Men's Player of the Month award for February. But India have representation in the women's category, with pacer Arundhati Reddy nominated.

India can field two playing XIs: Suryakumar Yadav hails country's talent poolIndia captain Suryakumar Yadav believes the country's T20 talent pool has grown so deep that multiple international-quality teams could be formed. He credits domestic cricket and the IPL for building India's remarkable strength in the format.

Was Gautam Gambhir behind Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma's Test retirements?Was Gautam Gambhir behind the Test retirements of Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma? India head coach Gautam Gambhir has hinted at the possible reason while speaking in an event in Kolkata.

India dominating cricket on all fronts: Mithali Raj calls it a dream come trueFormer India captain Mithali Raj believes Indian cricket is finally living up to its long-held potential. With the men's, women's and junior teams winning global titles, she says the country is witnessing a proud era of dominance.

Karnataka government clears Chinnaswamy stadium to host IPL 2026 matchesIPL 2026: Karnataka government has approved IPL matches at Chinnaswamy Stadium after an expert committee reviewed safety and preparedness measures at a meeting chaired by Home Minister G. Parameshwara.

Who is Max Dowman? Arsenal's wonderkid now Premier League's youngest scorerArsenal teenager Max Dowman made Premier League history with a stunning goal against Everton. At just 16 years and 73 days, the winger became the youngest goalscorer the league has ever seen.

Can Carrick bring the good times back to Manchester United? Rooney shares his stanceSeven wins from nine games have taken Manchester United from sixth to third under Michael Carrick, with Wayne Rooney and fans growing louder in their calls for him to be handed the job permanently.

Tried to make Gambhir laugh many times during T20 World Cup, reveals Suryakumar YadavSuryakumar Yadav revealed India's players tried repeatedly to make coach Gautam Gambhir laugh during the T20 World Cup 2026, finally succeeding after their title win in Ahmedabad on Sunday night.

Ahmedabad curse behind him, Suryakumar wishes he could replay 2023 World Cup finalSuryakumar Yadav says lifting the T20 World Cup 2026 trophy in Ahmedabad has finally put to rest the pain of the 2023 ODI World Cup final defeat at the same venue, the memory that haunted him most.

Watch: KKR injury worries grow as Harshit Rana spotted on crutches, IPL 2026 in doubtKKR's preparations for IPL 2026 could suffer a major blow as fast bowler Harshit Rana's injury concerns grow. The young pacer was recently seen on crutches, raising doubts over his availability for the upcoming season.

Ex-India coach Rahul Dravid proud of Sanju Samson's comeback journey in T20 World CupRahul Dravid has praised Sanju Samson's remarkable comeback during India's triumphant 2026 T20 World Cup campaign. The wicketkeeper-batter delivered decisive performances in the knockout stages and emerged as Player of the Tournament.

India winning the 2027 ODI World Cup is the ultimate goal: Shubman GillIndia captain Shubman Gill has set his sights on the 2027 ODI World Cup, calling it the ultimate target for the team after their heartbreak in the 2023 final. The skipper believes India have another shot at glory.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
